skill.name: context-management-context-restore
skill.description: Guides semantic context restoration for long-running AI projects, including retrieval, ranking, rehydration, and validation workflows.
skill.summary: Restore project context across AI sessions with semantic retrieval and structured validation guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found Markdown inline code, fenced examples, and descriptive text only. No executable implementation, network access, credential handling, malware behavior, or prompt injection attempt was found in SKILL.md. The skill is safe to publish as advisory documentation.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Markdown Code Spans Misread as Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command findings are Markdown inline code, Python pseudocode, and bash usage examples. They are not executable Ruby backtick calls and do not run commands by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are clearly Markdown formatting or fenced examples. No executable script file or runtime command invocation exists in the reviewed skill.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations contain the YAML description and a general product overview sentence. They do not reference MD5, SHA-1, insecure cipher use, or cryptographic implementation code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines contain descriptive text, not cryptographic algorithms or code. The later mention of cryptographic signatures is conceptual and not an insecure implementation.

content.value_statement: Long AI projects lose important decisions, constraints, and current state across sessions. This skill provides structured guidance for retrieving, ranking, rehydrating, and validating project context.
actual_capabilities: Defines input parameters for context source, project namespace, restoration mode, token budget, and relevance threshold.
Explains semantic retrieval using vector search, similarity thresholds, and relevance ranking.
Outlines rehydration patterns for prioritizing project overview, decisions, stack, recent work, and known issues.
Provides workflows for project resumption and cross-project knowledge transfer.
Describes validation, merge, provenance, caching, and integration patterns for context systems.
limitations: Provides guidance and pseudocode, not a working context restoration application.
Does not include implementation files for vector storage, embeddings, or command-line tooling.
Requires users to supply their own context database, project metadata, and validation rules.
Mentions an implementation playbook resource, but only SKILL.md is present in this package.

use_cases.title: Resume a Long Project
use_cases.description: Recover recent decisions, architecture notes, known issues, and active tasks before continuing work in a new AI session.

use_cases.title: Design Context Retrieval
use_cases.description: Plan vector search, relevance scoring, token budgeting, and validation steps for a custom memory restoration pipeline.

use_cases.title: Transfer Project Knowledge
use_cases.description: Map relevant decisions and reusable patterns from one project into another while preserving provenance and constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Resumption Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to help me restore context for project [name]. Summarize the project goal, recent work, decisions, blockers, and next actions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Incremental Context Loading
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design an incremental context restoration plan for [project]. Include source priorities, relevance thresholds, token budget, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Context Versions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to compare two context snapshots for [project]. Identify conflicts, missing decisions, stale assumptions, and a merge plan.

prompt_templates.title: Architect a Restoration System
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a semantic context restoration architecture. Include retrieval, ranking, provenance, integrity checks, caching, and failure handling.

output_examples.input: Restore context for a web platform after a two-week break.
output: A resumption summary with current objective, recent changes, active risks, and next actions.
A prioritized context list covering architecture decisions, stack details, open issues, and recent agent work.
Validation checks that compare restored context against the current repository state.

output_examples.input: Design an incremental restoration workflow for a token-limited session.
output: A staged loading plan that starts with project overview and active tasks.
A relevance scoring approach using semantic similarity, recency, and decision impact.
A fallback plan for conflicts, stale context, and missing source data.

best_practices: Start with the current user goal, then restore only context that supports that goal.
Validate restored decisions against the current repository or source of truth before acting on them.
Track provenance for restored context so users can see where each important fact came from.
anti_patterns: Do not load every historical note when a focused subset can answer the current task.
Do not treat semantic similarity scores as proof that context is still correct.
Do not merge conflicting project memories without recording the conflict and resolution.

faq.question: Does this skill restore context automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, workflows, and pseudocode for designing or operating context restoration.

faq.question: What tools does it assume?
faq.answer: It mentions vector databases, embeddings, RAG pipelines, and multi-agent workflows, but it does not require a specific vendor.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is tool-agnostic and can support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it include production code?
faq.answer: No. The file contains conceptual guidance, Python-style pseudocode, and example command syntax only.

faq.question: How should I choose a token budget?
faq.answer: Start with the smallest budget that covers goals, constraints, decisions, recent work, and known risks.

faq.question: How do I reduce stale context risk?
faq.answer: Use recency checks, repository validation, source provenance, and explicit conflict resolution before relying on restored context.
